content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 7, 1990


Stanley J. Cohen
21731 Saluda
Huntington Beach, CA  92646

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-89-720

Dear Mr. Cohen:

     You have written a letter to us commenting about our Advice
Letter Nos. A-89-441 and A-89-452 which responded to questions
posed by Teresa Craigie of Pillsbury, Madison and Sutro, and Gail
Hutton, City Attorney for the City of Huntington Beach. You also
expressed your opinion that certain public officials in Huntington
Beach were not acting in accordance with the advice in that
letter.

     Ms. Craigie, representing the Huntington Beach Company,
disagreed with the advice in that letter and requested that the
Commission review that advice. The Commission directed that a
memorandum explaining the issues involved in the validation
of gifts of memberships to country clubs or golf clubs. I sent
you a copy of the memorandum which discussed the issues. The
Commission has decided to take the matter under submission. The
Commission granted Ms. Craigie's request that she be permitted to
submit written comments. The Commission has not rendered its
decision regarding the advice contained in the letter.

     If you so desire, I will be glad to keep you informed of any
further developments on this subject. If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Jeevan S. Ahuja
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSA:aa


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 4, 1990


Stanley J. Cohen
21731 Saluda Circle
Huntington Beach, CA  92646

Dear Mr. Cohen:

You had written a letter to us about our response to the
questions posed by Teresa Craigie of Pillsbury, Madison and Sutro,
and Gail Hutton, City Attorney for the City of Huntington Beach.
The issues discussed in our response and the general issue of
valuation of gifts of passes to country clubs is being considered
by the Commission on January 9, 1990.

Enclosed for your information is the memorandum which
discusses the issue and requests direction from the Commission on
some of the questions raised in the memorandum. The Commission
welcomes comments from the public. If you have any questions,
please call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel, Legal Division

JA/jt
Enclosure

ASSET LOCATORS, INC.
AA 007764


November 10, 1989


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Your File Nos. A-89-441 and A-89-452

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Your "Requests for Advice" letter dated October 4, 1989,
addressed to Teresa Craige of Pillsbury, Madison and Sutro and
Gail Hutton, City Attorney for the City of Huntington Beach, is
being totally ignored.

On page 6, paragraph "2. Valuation of the VIP Membership for
purposes of Disqualification." you advised that the value of a
VIP membership in the Seacliff Country Club owned and operated by
the Huntington Beach Company "for purposes of disqualification"
from participating in and voting on matters concerning the Company "is the value based on the actual use during the past 12
months, ..., plus $30 per month ....".

Therefore, if a public official does NOT return or transfer
his or her VIP membership card 12 months prior to the decision,
the value of that membership card exceeds $250, thereby excluding
that official from voting on matters concerning that "donor" (the
Huntington Beach Company) per section 87103(e) of the Government
Code.

There are other public officials of the Huntington Beach
Planning Commission that are willing to testify that certain
Planning Commissioners as well as other city government members
in Huntington Beach:

1. have received their VIP membership cards at least 12
months prior to the decision
2. have retained and USED these cards
3. have read and continue to disregard your "advice
letter"
4. have participated in discussions leading to votes on
matters concerning this donor.

Also, when these officials or other concerned recipients of
these cards have sought the advice of the City Attorney and/or
her staff, they have been told that your "advice" is not correct
and is not relevant to the daily operation of the City.

13211 GARDEN GROVE BLVD., SUITE 204 • GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92643 • (714) 740-1600

Can the California Fair Political Practices Commission,
through your good offices, notify the City of Huntington Beach
and the City Attorney that your advice is NOT to be taken lightly
and without any consequences? The concerned citizens of Hunting-
ton Beach deserve no less!

Yours most sincerely,

Stanley J. Cohen
21731 Saluda Circle
Huntington Beach, CA 92646


SJC/xc
